title: Mixtral 8x7B
description: The first open-weights Mixtral sparse mixture-of-experts model with 46.7 billion total parameters, about 12.9 billion active per token, and a 32,768-token context window under Apache 2.0.
openingSummary: Mixtral 8x7B is Mistral AI's first published sparse mixture-of-experts open-weights release. A router selects two of eight feed-forward experts per token, so the model stores a large total parameter budget while keeping active compute closer to a 12.9-billion-parameter dense stack.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Mixtral 8x7B is a decoder-only sparse mixture-of-experts language model from Mistral AI, released on 2023-12-11 in the Mixtral of experts announcement. Mistral describes it as a sparse mixture-of-experts network where, at every layer and for every token, a router chooses two of eight expert groups to process the token and combine their output additively. The release ships under the Apache 2.0 license as open weights.

inputsAndOutputs.title: Inputs And Outputs
inputsAndOutputs.body: Mixtral 8x7B is a text-in, text-out model. Official release materials list English, French, Italian, German, and Spanish support, a 32,768-token context window, and strong code-generation behavior in the base checkpoint. This page keeps those figures because they are the conservative operating points repeated across Mistral's release post and model card.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.body: As a decoder-only sparse mixture-of-experts model, Mixtral 8x7B keeps the familiar transformer decoder skeleton but replaces each dense feed-forward block with routed experts. Mistral's release post states that the router selects two experts per token from eight distinct parameter groups, which is why total parameter count and active parameter count diverge. The diagram below stays at the decoder level—attention, normalization, and a sparse expert routing block—so it teaches token-to-router-to-selected-experts flow without turning every internal block name into an extra claim.

importantModules.title: Important Modules
importantModules.body: The registry-backed modules below are the smallest set worth opening first when you want to connect this Mixtral page to the site's module reference. Mixture-of-experts routing is the defining feed-forward behavior here, while grouped-query attention, RMS normalization, SwiGLU, and rotary position encoding anchor the high-level stack shown above.

training.title: Training
training.body: Mistral's release post describes pre-training on data extracted from the open web and training experts and routers simultaneously. The same announcement also releases an instruction-tuned Mixtral 8x7B Instruct variant, but this page does not restate fine-tuning recipe details beyond what those primary sources publish.

practicalNotes.title: Practical Notes
practicalNotes.body: Read Mixtral 8x7B as the earlier open-weights sparse expert release in the Mixtral line. Public materials cite 46.7 billion total parameters with about 12.9 billion active per token, which is the main practical distinction when you compare memory residency and inference cost against a dense model with the same total parameter budget.

architectureGraph.alt: Mixtral 8x7B architecture diagram with token embeddings, rotary position encoding, a repeated decoder block containing grouped-query attention, RMSNorm, and a sparse expert routing block that selects two of eight experts per token, then an output projection and softmax.

rmsnormAttention.label: RMSNorm
rmsnormAttention.summary: Normalizes the residual stream before the attention step

decoderAttention.label:
Grouped-Query
Attention
decoderAttention.summary: Each token reads earlier context under the model's attention mask

rmsnormMoe.label: RMSNorm
rmsnormMoe.summary: Normalizes the stream before the sparse expert routing block

moeRouting.label:
Expert
Router
moeRouting.summary: Selects two of eight feed-forward experts per token and combines their outputs

ropeInject.summary: Applies token states together with rotary position information before the stack

rope.summary: Rotary position encoding gives the attention stack token order information
